Hemp more sustainable than cotton/polyester: low water/pesticide use, natural antimicrobial, durable (lasts longer), less resource-intensive production, non-toxic dyes/chemicals. Good sustainability rating overall.

Organic hemp production challenges: limited availability of certified organic, limited infrastructure, higher costs, difficult fiber processing, blended materials needed, low consumer awareness/education.
Organic Hemp Certification Program (OTA/HIA) in US; Patagonia partnership with Kentucky farmers for organic hemp pilot. Consumer demand drives adoption of sustainable practices by manufacturers.
Companies using 100% organic hemp: Jungmaven, Rawganique, Hoodlamb (hemp/organic cotton), WAMA (hemp/organic cotton underwear), Hempest. Committed to sustainability and ethical sourcing.

Consumer actions for sustainability: choose eco-friendly brands, research practices, support organic products, buy less/better quality, advocate via social media/petitions, voicing demand for sustainable options.
